commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=ccdd6a344ee8d4a4ba26a1a74cd624da87e7a72e)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Gemini fnm helper process output capture, drains stderr, adds SIGKILL escalation, and updates the expired-token fnm OAuth refresh test to simulate a child that keeps stdout open.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: current main waits for the fnm helper and then drains stdout to EOF without a deadline, so a descendant retaining stdout can block discovery indefinitely. I did not run a live Gemini/fnm setup in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The contributor needs to repair the subprocess drain/test portability and add real behavior proof; ClawSweeper should not queue a repair while contributor-owned proof is missing.

target.title: Fix Gemini fnm package discovery pipe hangs
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #1534.

Gemini fnm package discovery waited for stdout EOF after `fnm` exited. If a descendant inherited stdout, the direct helper could be done while discovery still hung. This captures stdout incrementally, drains stderr so helpers cannot block on a full pipe, and bounds timeout cleanup with SIGKILL escalation.

The regression keeps the existing expired-token fnm OAuth refresh path, but makes fake `fnm exec -- npm root -g` leave stdout open through a child process. The refresh now completes from the parent output instead of waiting for inherited EOF.

## Proof

- `swiftc -swift-version 6 -parse Sources/CodexBarCore/Providers/Gemini/GeminiStatusProbe.swift Tests/CodexBarTests/GeminiStatusProbeAPITests.swift Tests/CodexBarTests/GeminiTestEnvironment.swift`

I also attempted focused SwiftPM tests locally, but SwiftPM hung while downloading Sparkle's binary artifact before compilation. CI should run the focused regression under normal package resolution.
target.author: kiranmagic7
